tldr: Feature engineering pipeline leveraging ESPN box scores, point-difference team quality, ELO with season replays, Massey ordinals, and tournament experience proxies to enhance a baseline model.
summary: The author primarily relied on last year's baseline code, focusing almost entirely on feature engineering and data curation rather than model architecture changes. Key improvements included integrating scraped ESPN box scores for consistent rebound and turnover metrics, and redefining team quality using point difference, missed free throws, and location instead of binary outcomes. The validation strategy involved hand-picked cutoffs (e.g., <33% win rate filter) and removing play-in games to reduce training outliers. Score gains were driven by advanced ranking systems (Massey ordinals, ELO with season replays), Poisson-based win expectations, and an exponentially weighted moving average of historical tournament reach.
extensive_summary:
## Data & Filtering
Built on last year's code, the author scraped ESPN box scores for consistent rebound/turnover data (+0.002–0.005 Brier). Teams with <33% regular season win rate were filtered via validation cutoffs. Play-in games were removed as top MSE outliers, noting seed 10 vs 10 does not imply equal strength.

## Feature Engineering
Team quality shifted to point difference + missed free throws + location, weighted by seeds/DayNum. Conference quality used inter-conference games only. ELO used pointdiff minus expected margin with n=5 season replays. Massey ordinals at DayNum=133 used worst_rank(POM, WLK, MOR), with a men-to-women proxy model. A season-by-season Poisson model (`y = 2**num_wins`) predicted expected wins. Historical tournament reach was tracked via statistics and EMA (half-life=2.5 years) for experience. A "better team always wins" bracket feature used majority vote of seed, quality, and ELO.
preprocessing: Scraped ESPN API data for consistent rebound/turnover metrics
Filtered teams with <33% regular season win rate using hand-picked validation cutoffs
Removed play-in games from training to reduce MSE outliers
Season-by-season fitting to prevent data leakage
feature_engineering: Team quality: point difference + missed free throws adjustment + location factor
Sample weights based on seeds and DayNum
ELO with season replays (n=5) using pointdiff minus expected margin
Massey ordinals at DayNum=133 (worst_rank of POM, WLK, MOR)
Proxy model predicting men's Massey ranks for women's features
Poisson count model for expected tournament wins (y = 2**num_wins)
Statistics of reached rounds in previous tournaments
EMA of reached rounds (half-life=2.5 years) as tournament experience proxy
Better team always wins bracket feature (majority vote of seed, quality, ELO)
Expected number of wins as feature
what_worked: Removing play-in games from training improved validation metrics the most
Switching team quality to point difference + missed free throws + location was the best feature
EMA(y) with half-life=2.5 years proved incredibly strong for tournament experience
ESPN box scores provided a 0.002-0.005 Brier boost
critical_findings: Play-in games are significant outliers in training MSE and should be excluded.
Seed 10 vs Seed 10 matchups do not imply equal team strength.
